*HN Digest — 2026-07-11*

_Heavy AI day — GPT-5.6 launches with a math-proof victory lap, a solo dev fits GLM 5.2 on a laptop, Apple sues OpenAI._

1. **[AI & agents]** [GPT-5.6](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-6/) — 1522 pts · 1082 comments
Why it matters: Flagship refresh — three sizes (Sol/Terra/Luna), adaptive reasoning, claimed 13-pt lead over Fable 5 on Agents' Last Exam at ~¼ the cost.
HN take: "About 20 thinking/model levels in this family — very complex to pick the right one. Feels like it needs an auto mode that picks best model and thinking per problem." — _mchusma_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48849066)

2. **[AI & agents]** [Show HN: GLM 5.2 on my slow computer](https://github.com/JustVugg/colibri) — 843 pts · 208 comments
Why it matters: Fits GLM 5.2 into a 12-core/25GB box by streaming weights VRAM-in/out — frontier-model-on-your-laptop is closer than cloud vendors want you to believe.
HN take: "Moving mmap'd bytes in and out of VRAM is way cheap compared to compute. With pipelining/double-buffering I end up compute-bound, not memory-bound." — _kodablah_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48842459)

3. **[Business & legal]** [Apple sues OpenAI over stolen trade secrets](https://9to5mac.com/2026/07/10/apple-sues-openai-trade-secret-theft/) — 876 pts · 440 comments
Why it matters: Suit alleges ex-employees walked out with GBs of internal data. Sets precedent for the AI talent-mobility fights coming this cycle.
HN take: "OpenAI is a company built on copyright violation — treating laws as things for little people is in the corporate DNA. Apple have deep enough pockets to actually sue." — _Lio_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48865019)

4. **[Infra & devtools]** [Postgres rewritten in Rust, passes 100% of regression tests](https://github.com/malisper/pgrust) — 793 pts · 707 comments
Why it matters: pgrust ports Postgres to Rust and clears the full regression suite. Port is real; single-author vibe-coded maintenance is the open question.
HN take: "The answer might be a proxy that splits traffic to a known Postgres and the rewrite, comparing outcomes over a month. If it's identical, it's probably fine." — _Havoc_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48841676)

5. **[Security & policy]** [The glass backbone: Army logistics will break in the next war](https://mwi.westpoint.edu/the-glass-backbone-why-the-armys-logistics-will-break-in-the-next-war/) — 454 pts · 644 comments
Why it matters: US Army logistics optimized into a glass tail — centralized, drone-vulnerable — with "tooth-to-tail" thinking now making the tail the primary target.
HN take: "Since the fall of the USSR the center of the US military has been contractor margins. Why get Congress to pay for cheap drones when they'll pay $4M for Patriots?" — _causality0_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48845442)

6. **[Product & Show HN]** [Show HN: 18 Words](https://18words.com/) — 1093 pts · 348 comments
Why it matters: Pattern-guessing word game — hit 132k players day one, same-night update from HN feedback. Fast-iteration launch worth studying.
Author claim: "The game had 132,411 players yesterday. Overnight I shipped a share feature and moved from one-strike-you're-out to a full 18-word run with a final score." — _pompomsheep_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48845049)

7. **[Science]** [GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra proves the Cycle Double Cover Conjecture](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/04d1d1e4-bc75-476a-97cf-49055cd98d31/cdc_proof.pdf) — 415 pts · 324 comments
Why it matters: First non-counterexample machine proof of an open problem — concise, but appears to lean on a clever trick, not new theory.
HN take: "The one thing AI hasn't managed: an autonomous theory-building proof — one that needs substantial new theory (30+ pages) to crack an open problem." — _ak_111_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48863490)
